---
title: "011 — Who switches first? Survival analysis of regime transitions"
date: 2026-06-12
---

## Question

Experiment 008 gave each state an energy-regime calendar. Here we ask what
*timing* tells us: do richer, more urban states switch regimes sooner (a
resource-driven transition), or does a common national wave sweep states
along regardless of local conditions? (Background: *Regime-Switch Survival*
card in the [guide](guide.qmd).)

## Setup

```{python}
import duckdb
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from complexity_lab.config import settings
from complexity_lab.complexity.regimes import fit_fuel_regimes
from complexity_lab.complexity.survival import (
    build_risk_set, discrete_hazard_model, kaplan_meier,
)

con = duckdb.connect(str(settings.db_path), read_only=True)
panel = con.execute(
    "SELECT state_code, year, petrol_share, diesel_share, cng_share, ev_share, hybrid_share, "
    "real_pc_income_inr, real_gsdp_growth_pct, broad_credit_per_capita_inr "
    "FROM experiment_state_year "
    "WHERE year < (SELECT MAX(year) FROM experiment_state_year) "
    "ORDER BY state_code, year").df()
regimes = fit_fuel_regimes(panel)
COVS = ["real_pc_income_inr", "real_gsdp_growth_pct", "broad_credit_per_capita_inr"]
risk = build_risk_set(regimes["calendar"], panel, COVS)
print(f"{risk['state_code'].nunique()} states at risk, "
      f"{int(risk['switched'].sum())} first switches observed")
```

## Survival curve — when does the market move?

```{python}
km = kaplan_meier(risk)
fig = px.line(km, x="year", y="survival", markers=True,
              title="Share of states still in their original regime")
fig.add_bar(x=km["year"], y=km["hazard"], name="yearly hazard", opacity=0.3)
fig
```

## The hazard model

```{python}
model = discrete_hazard_model(risk, COVS)
tbl = model["table"].round(3)
tbl
```

```{python}
plot = tbl.reset_index(names="covariate")
px.bar(plot, x="covariate", y="odds_ratio_per_sd", text="p_value",
       title="Odds ratio per 1 SD (>1 accelerates the switch)"
       ).add_hline(y=1.0, line_dash="dot")
```

## Finding

```{python}
print(f"events = {model['n_events']}, state-years at risk = {model['n_obs']}, "
      f"pseudo-R² = {model['pseudo_r2']:.2f}")
```

With 15 first switches, real income, real GSDP growth, and broad personal-credit
depth are not statistically distinguishable from zero. Credit depth has the
largest positive point estimate, but uncertainty remains wide. The
**calendar-time trend is overwhelming**
(odds rising ~1.8× per period-SD, p < 0.001): switches concentrate in the
2021–2022 window across rich and poor states alike. Combined with experiment
009 (the co-adoption network beating geography and economics at predicting
adoption timing), the picture is consistent: **India's fuel-regime transition
is a nationally synchronized wave** — driven by policy and product cycles —
rather than a local-resources race. The honest caveats: events are few, the
time trend partially absorbs covariate growth, and yearly grain hides
within-year sequencing. District-level data would turn this into the full Cox
frailty model the blueprint specifies.
